channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump DOJ mass-deletes info on Jan. 6 riot cases, including violent assaults on cops
item.description: The Trump Department of Justice purged government news releases with information about prosecutions of rioters who attacked the U.S. Capitol and assaulted law enforcement on Jan. 6, 2021.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump Department of Justice purged government news releases with information about prosecutions of rioters who attacked the U.S. Capitol and assaulted law enforcement on Jan. 6, 2021.

(Image credit: Joseph Prezioso)
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach

item.title: Which players will make the cut? The U.S. World Cup soccer roster is revealed today
item.description: Some veterans from the last World Cup — like forward Christian Pulisic and midfielders Weston McKennie and Tyler Adams — are certain to make the cut. The full roster will be announced at 3 p.m. ET.
item.content:encoded:
Some veterans from the last World Cup — like forward Christian Pulisic and midfielders Weston McKennie and Tyler Adams — are certain to make the cut. The full roster will be announced at 3 p.m. ET.

(Image credit: Eduardo Munoz Alvarez)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Why the French Open is named after Roland Garros, who didn't play tennis
item.description: Roland Garros, a WWI-era pilot, is credited with revolutionizing aerial combat. The major Paris tennis tournament was named after him in 1928, a decade after his death in combat.
item.content:encoded:
Roland Garros, a WWI-era pilot, is credited with revolutionizing aerial combat. The major Paris tennis tournament was named after him in 1928, a decade after his death in combat.

(Image credit: Branger/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: An exceptionally early heat wave shatters records and brings deaths in Europe
item.description: Temperature records are breaking and triggering government warnings, with London hitting 95 degrees the past two days. Experts say unpredictable and extreme weather is becoming more frequent.
item.content:encoded:
Temperature records are breaking and triggering government warnings, with London hitting 95 degrees the past two days. Experts say unpredictable and extreme weather is becoming more frequent.

(Image credit: Kin Cheung)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How the Trump administration uses the Bible to justify military invasions and immigration raids
item.description: President Trump isn't much of a Bible-quoter, but some members of his administration are, and they have used Scripture to frame controversial policies as justified by the Good Book.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump isn't much of a Bible-quoter, but some members of his administration are, and they have used Scripture to frame controversial policies as justified by the Good Book. 

(Image credit: Pool photo)
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: Prospects fade for imminent end to Iran war as attacks restart
item.description: Israel says it will intensify attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon and U.S. military struck Iranian boats and missile launch sites as envoys continued negotiations for a deal that would end the three-month war.
item.content:encoded:
Israel says it will intensify attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon and U.S. military struck Iranian boats and missile launch sites as envoys continued negotiations for a deal that would end the three-month war.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: U.S. strikes Iran. And, immigration courts use new tactic to speed up deportations
item.description: The U.S. military has launched new attacks on Iran while talks to end the conflict are ongoing. And, the Department of Justice is using a new tactic in immigration courts to accelerate deportations.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military has launched new attacks on Iran while talks to end the conflict are ongoing. And, the Department of Justice is using a new tactic in immigration courts to accelerate deportations.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Therapists are using AI to take notes. Is it a useful tool or a breach of trust?
item.description: New companies are selling artificial intelligence assistance to mental health therapists. The AI tools can help with administration and recordkeeping, but some patients worry about their privacy.
item.content:encoded:
New companies are selling artificial intelligence assistance to mental health therapists. The AI tools can help with administration and recordkeeping, but some patients worry about their privacy.

(Image credit: Fiordaliso)
item.dc:creator: Windsor Johnston

item.title: In West Texas, an unlikely alliance stands against extending the border wall
item.description: An unusual coalition of people across the political spectrum have banded together to rally against a border wall in the Big Bend.
item.content:encoded:
An unusual coalition of people across the political spectrum have banded together to rally against a border wall in the Big Bend.

(Image credit: Carlos Morales)
item.dc:creator: Carlos Morales

item.title: Immigration courts are using a new tactic to speed up deportations
item.description: The Justice Department is moving up the court hearings for hundreds of immigrants and scheduling them for mass hearings. If they don't show up, they could be ordered deported.
item.content:encoded:
The Justice Department is moving up the court hearings for hundreds of immigrants and scheduling them for mass hearings. If they don't show up, they could be ordered deported.

(Image credit: Charly Triballeau)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo
